decisions.title: Prioritize PyRunner Stability vs Feature Velocity
decisions.context: ENG-10619 blocking production workflows. PyRunner 'unavailable' responses creating stuck states. Impacts Python Rules (Build the Moat) reliability. Need to decide: halt feature work for stability sprint or continue parallel tracks.

arguments.for: Python Rules is moat differentiator. Production instability undermines trust. One-week stability sprint prevents customer churn.
arguments.against: Canvas and Explore V2 timelines already tight. Can isolate PyRunner team without halting all feature work. Risk over-rotating on edge case.

decisions.title: Accelerate Sift Edge GUI vs Embedded Workflows
decisions.context: ENG-10376 (Edge GUI channel search) and multiple alignment point tickets (ENG-6231, ENG-7789) signal Edge getting heavier. Strategy says 'Embed Deeper' but Edge GUI expansion might conflict. Clarify: is Edge a standalone product or embedding layer?

arguments.for: Edge customers need self-service. GUI features unlock DoD/mil buyers who want on-prem. Competitive moat if Edge becomes full analysis platform.
arguments.against: Diverges from 'Embed Deeper' pillar. Maintenance burden of two UIs. Canvas should be the primary interface; Edge should be data pipeline.

decisions.title: Canvas Comments/History Scope for Q2 Release
decisions.context: DES-164 (Canvas report comments) critical for 'Decisions Captured' metric. But also Live Mode (DES-197), Unified Sync (DES-196), and E2 context menu (DES-151) in flight. Can't ship all. Need to cut scope or push Canvas collaboration features to Q3.

arguments.for: Comments/history directly drive North Star (captures decision rationale). Defer Live Mode to Q3; it's nice-to-have vs collaboration essentials.
arguments.against: Live Mode differentiates vs static tools. Customers already asking for real-time collab. Comments can be MVP'd later if sync works well.

decisions.title: TRMC IDIQ Sole-Source Resource Allocation
decisions.context: CAGR-24 targeting test-center funding unlock. High-value government channel. Competes with commercial GTM (Morgan Stanley CAGR-29, Starpath/Globalstar partnerships). Decide: dedicate BD resource to TRMC or maintain broad commercial focus.
decisions.blockedBy: Awaiting legal review on sole-source eligibility and procurement timeline clarity from TRMC contact

arguments.for: IDIQ unlocks multi-year recurring gov revenue. Test centers are repeatable wedge. Aligns with Edge (on-prem) strategy.
arguments.against: Sole-source cycles are 9-12 months. Morgan Stanley could close in Q2 with higher ACV. Spreading BD too thin risks losing both.

decisions.title: Session Timeout Policy: 15min vs User Experience
decisions.context: ENG-9304 proposes 15min session timeout for security. Conflicts with long-running analysis workflows in Explore/Canvas. Need policy: strict timeout, context-aware (active analysis = extend), or opt-in for high-security customers.

arguments.for: DoD customers require strict session policies (compliance). Recent Vercel security incident (ENG-10611) shows need for tighter controls. 15min is industry standard.
arguments.against: Analysts run 2-hour explorations. Forced re-auth breaks flow, reduces Decisions Captured. Context-aware timeout (activity-based) balances security and UX.

keyResults.name: Avg Sessions Per User Per Week

timeAudit.weekSummary: 38% free time below 50% target. 3 one-on-ones consolidated to reclaim 1.5hrs. Cancelled recurring platform sync (low value). Added 2hr Thursday focus block for TRMC strategy review.
